title: alpinia aenea
intro: Alpinia aenea, also known as red ginger or lesser galangal, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a type of rhizome that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Alpinia aenea include reducing nausea and vomiting, alleviating menstrual cramps, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Alpinia aenea is often prepared as a tea, powder, or oil, and is also used in traditional cooking, particularly in Southeast Asian cuisine.
